name: DynForms.View

values.name: viewForm
values.comment: Render form as Elm HTML

name: DynForms.JsonDecode

values.name: decodeString
values.comment: Decode a JSON string to its corresponding form object

values.name: decodeValue
values.comment: Decode a JSON value to its corresponding form object

values.name: fieldInfoDecoder
values.comment: Decodes JSON to a FieldInfo object.

values.name: formDecoder
values.comment: Decodes JSON to a form object

values.name: layoutDecoder
values.comment: Decodes JSON to a layout object

values.name: validatorDecoder
values.comment: Decodes JSON to a (validator, errorString) tuple

name: DynForms.Validation
comment:
Validators objects represent an specific validation operation. DynForms
do not support custom validations since that implies putting functions inside
the model.


# Basic validators

@docs maxValue, minValue, maxLength, minLength


# Additional options

@docs withMsg, defaultErrorMsg

values.name: defaultErrorMsg
values.comment: Return the default error message for the given validator

values.name: maxLength
values.comment: Sets the maximum length value a field can assume

values.name: maxValue
values.comment: Sets the maximum numerical value a field can assume

values.name: minLength
values.comment: Sets the minimum length value a field can assume

values.name: minValue
values.comment: Sets the minimum numerical value a field can assume

values.name: withMsg
values.comment:
Sets the error string used in the validator.

This function is designed to be used in a pipeline as in

    validators =
        [ minValue 0 |> withMsg "Value must be positive" ]

name: DynForms.JsonEncode
comment:
Functions that encode a form or parts of the form to JSON.

The most useful functions are the encodeValue that encodes the complete form
and encodeValueData that only encodes the current state of form data.

@docs encodeValue, encodeString


# Other partial encoders

Partial encoders do not implement the convenience encode-to-string function.

@docs encodeFieldInfo, encodeLayout

values.name: encodeFieldInfo
values.comment: Encodes a FieldInfo object to a value

values.name: encodeLayout
values.comment: Encodes a Layout object to a value

values.name: encodeString
values.comment:
Encode a form to a JSON string.

The first parameter is an integer value describing the indentation level. (It
can be safely set to 0.)

values.name: encodeValue
values.comment: Encode a form to a JSON value.

name: DynForms.Layout

values.name: forceLinearLayout
values.comment: Converts any form layout to a linear layout

values.name: forceTableLayout
values.comment: Converts any form layout to a linear table layout

values.name: linearLayout
values.comment: Declares a linear layout

values.name: tableLayout
values.comment: Declares a linear layout

name: DynForms.Field
comment:
# Field types

@docs stringField, textField, boolField, hiddenField, intField, floatField


## Constructor helpers

The functions bellow create modified versions of the field setting up one of
its parameters. They are useful for initializing the field object using the
pipeline notation as in

    myField =
        intField "field-id"
            |> label "Number"
            |> placeholder "42"
            |> default "0"

@docs label, placeholder, help, default, validators

values.name: boolField
values.comment:
Boolean fields hold a boolean value and are usually represented by a
checkbox.

values.name: default
values.comment:
Sets the default value of a field.

This pre-fills the field with some value. If this option is enabled, the
placeholder should not appear unless the user deletes the field content.

values.name: floatField
values.comment: Float fields hold numeric data that can have decimal places.

values.name: help
values.comment:
Defines a help string that is usually displayed bellow the field input
element.

Help strings are useful UI elements that can be used to provide additional
information to the user on how to fill the field or any other type of
clarification.

values.name: hiddenField
values.comment: Hidden fields hold a string value that is hidden from the user.

values.name: intField
values.comment: Int fields hold numeric data as whole numbers.

values.name: label
values.comment:
Sets the label text of a field.

The label is usually place just above or to the left of a field input box
to tell the user what kind of information should be filled in.

values.name: placeholder
values.comment:
Sets the placeholder text of a field.

The placeholder is a text that is displayed inside the input field and is
replaced by the user input as soon as the user starts typing. A common design
choice is to replace the label with a placeholder. DynForms treats both
independently.

values.name: stringField
values.comment: String fields represents a simple line of string input.

values.name: textField
values.comment:
Text fields are similar to regular string fields, but uses a textarea as
the default input element.

values.name: validators
values.comment:
Defines a list of validators for a field declaration.

Each validator is declared using the functions in the DynForms.Validators
module.

name: DynForms
comment:
Dynamic forms usually start as some JSON sent by the backend. Elm takes control
to present to render the form, interact with the user and perform basic
validation. Data can be serialized and sent back to the server for further
validation and persistence.

If you want to generate a form from JSON, take a look at the
DynForms.JsonDecode and DynForms.JsonEncode modules. You can also construct
form objects in Elm. A form is defined by a series of fields and a layout.

    import DynForms exposing (form)
    import DynForms.Field exposing (..)
    import DynForms.Validation exposing (..)

    sampleForm : Form
    sampleForm =
        form "/api/form/1"
            [ stringField "name"
                |> label "Name"
                |> placeholder "Full name"
                |> validators [ maxLength 200 ]
            , intField "age"
                |> label "Age"
                |> help "Your age"
                |> validators [ minValue 0, maxValue 120 ]
            ]


# Form creation

@docs Form, form


# Basic data types

The DynForms module also defines a few auxiliary data types.

@docs FieldType, FormLayout, FieldInfo, Validator


# Field constructors and introspection

A field is declared using functions in the DynForms.Fields module. This module
defines a few functions used to construct a field (e.g., label, help,
placeholder, etc). If you want to instrospect an specific field use one of the
functions bellow.

The `try*` functions return a Maybe while the `get*` functions return a definite
value.

@docs tryFieldInfoAt, getFieldLabelAt, tryFieldDefaultAt, tryFieldPlaceholderAt
@docs tryFieldHelpAt, tryFieldTypeAt, getFieldValidatorsAt


# Data access

The content of a field can be retrieved by a few data access functions.
Notice that those are not type safe functions and you should make sure that you
always use the right function to access each field.

@docs getStringAt, getBoolAt, getFloatAt, getIntAt


# Validators

Each field may define a list of validators that defines additional validation
routines that are not simply based on the content type (set up a minimum
value of a number, or the maximum length of a string, etc). Field validators
are declared in the DynForms.Validation module.


# View Functions

View functions are declared in the DynForms.View module. Unless you are trying
someting fancy, you really should care only about the viewForm() function.


# Elm Archtecture

DynForms follows a very standard Elm archtecture. `Form` can be safely used
inside a model. It also defines the view function on the `DynForms.View`
module. You should also use `DynForms.Msg` and `DynForms.updateForm` functions.

@docs Msg, updateForm

You may want to check the self-contained example at
<https://github.com/fabiommendes/elm-dynamic-forms/blob/master/example/Example.elm>

unions.name: FieldType
unions.comment:
Field type.

Enumerate the possible field types.

unions.name: FormLayout
unions.comment:
Represents a layout for the form elements.

The Layout describes how form elements will be placed on the page when creating
a view. Check the `DynForms.Layout` module for more options.

unions.name: Validator
unions.comment:
Validator

Describe the possible validation routines. Error strings can be set separately
from the validator. See `DynForms.Validation` for more options.

aliases.name: FieldInfo
aliases.comment:
Stores basic information about a field.

Use the functions on `DynForms.Field` to create FieldInfo objets unless you
know what your are doing.
aliases.type: { id : String, fieldType : FieldType, dataType : FieldDataType, label : Maybe String, placeholder : Maybe String, default : Maybe FieldData, help : Maybe String, validators : List ( Validator, String ) }

aliases.name: Form
aliases.comment:
Basic form type.

The Form type represents a full form in the application including the filled in
values, declarations of field types and behaviors and the form layout.

Forms can be safely included in a model. It replicates a standard Elm
archtecture and provides standard functions and messages such as updateForm,
viewForm and FormMsg.

You should create new forms using the DynForms.form constructor.
aliases.type: { action : String, ajax : Bool, fields : Dict String FieldInfo, layout : FormLayout, state : FormState }

values.name: form
values.comment:
Creates a new form.

You should pass the address to the endpoint that handles the form and a list of
fields.

values.name: getBoolAt
values.comment:
Returns the boolean value stored in the field with the given id of a form
object.

Invalid data is silently coerced to False.

values.name: getFieldLabelAt
values.comment:
Get field label for field with the given id, if it exists and label is
defined.

values.name: getFieldValidatorsAt
values.comment:
Get list of validators for the given field. If field does not exist,
return an empty list.

values.name: getFloatAt
values.comment:
Returns the float value stored in the field with the given id of a form
object.

Invalid data is silently coerced to 0.0.

values.name: getIntAt
values.comment:
Returns the integer value stored in the field with the given id of a form
object.

Invalid data is silently coerced to 0.

values.name: getStringAt
values.comment:
Returns the string stored in the field with the given id of a form object.

Invalid data (e.g., non-string values) is silently converted to string or
coerced to an empty string.

    getStringAt "name" form == "R2D2"

values.name: tryFieldDefaultAt
values.comment:
Get field default value for field with the given id, if it exists and
default value is defined.

values.name: tryFieldHelpAt
values.comment:
Get field help string for field with the given id, if it exists and the
help string is defined.

values.name: tryFieldInfoAt
values.comment: Retrive information from a field if it exists

values.name: tryFieldPlaceholderAt
values.comment:
Get field placeholder for field with the given id, if it exists and
placeholder is defined.

values.name: tryFieldTypeAt
values.comment: Get field type for field with the given id, if it exists.

values.name: updateForm
values.comment: Update form using message.
